content: We can reframe the task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a base scaffold to anchor multiple-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hybrid system where iterative self-feedback loops progressively resolve cross-perspective solution inconsistencies to improve output quality without any supervised training or external data. This hybrid architecture leverages the strength of both approaches: Self-Refine's efficient no-training iterative improvement and self-reflection mechanisms' bias reduction from multiple problem-solving perspectives.
